mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payroll employment signals expanding economic activity, increasing corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Spearman rank correlation between 3-month change in PAYEMS and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is ≤ 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, tight monetary policy compresses valuation multiples and increases recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In any 3-year period where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4% for at least 6 months, the average 60-day forward SPX return is not negative, or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising retail sales and falling unemployment indicate robust consumer spending and income growth, jointly boosting corporate profits and S&P 500 returns over the near term.
failure_condition: In periods where RSAFS rises month-over-month and UNRATE falls month-over-month simultaneously, the median 20-day forward SPX return is not positive or the Sharpe ratio is ≤ 0.

mechanism_narrative: During housing market recoveries, rising housing starts lead positive economic sentiment and construction activity, translating into above-average S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: During the identified housing recovery regime, the average 5-day forward SPX return following a positive month-over-month change in HOUST is not statistically greater than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price inflation above a monthly threshold triggers expectations of monetary tightening, raising discount rates and depressing equity valuations in the very short term.
failure_condition: In instances where CPIAUCSL monthly change exceeds 0.5%, the 5-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the conditional mean return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero and industrial production accelerates, the combination of cheap credit and rising output signals a self-reinforcing expansion that boosts equity markets over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.5% and INDPRO rises month-over-month for 3 consecutive months, the average 60-day forward SPX return is not positive or the win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in real GDP indicates broad economic expansion, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and lifts S&P 500 valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between year-over-year GDP growth and subsequent 60-day SPX return is ≤ 0.03 over any 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A high unemployment rate above 9% indicates significant economic slack and weak consumer demand, weighing on corporate profits and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In months when UNRATE exceeds 9%, the 20-day forward SPX return is positive more than half the time, or the average return is not significantly less than the unconditional average (t-test p > 0.10).
